title: quercus brantii subsp. persica
intro: Quercus brantii subsp. persica, also known as Persian oak, is a subspecies of deciduous tree that is native to the mountainous regions of Iran, Turkey, and parts of the Middle East. The bark and leaves of the Persian oak contain astringent and anti-inflammatory compounds, which have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties. The plant is said to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, treating digestive issues, and acting as an antiseptic to prevent infections. Persian oak is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to take advantage of its medicinal and health benefits.
